claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to understand your situation. 

**First, let me ask you some basic questions:**

1. **Do you have a child or children you're claiming for?** If yes, please tell me:
   - Their name(s)
   - Their age(s)

The eligibility depends on the age of the child:
- Children under 16 generally qualify
- Children aged 16-19 may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children aged 20 or over generally don't qualify

Please share the details about your child or children, and I'll guide you through the rest of the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi, yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 17 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So you have one child, Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is 17 (between 16-19 years old), his eligibility for Child Benefit depends on whether he's in approved education or training. Let me navigate to the next step.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Now I need to know about Alex's current education or training status:

**Is Alex currently:**
1. **In education or training** (enrolled in a course/program), OR
2. **Recently left education** (within the last few months), OR
3. **Neither** (not in education/training and hasn't been recently)

Please let me know which situation applies to Alex.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: He's not in education or training anymore. He left school and he's working full-time now at the local supermarket.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So Alex left education and is now working full-time at a supermarket.

Let me ask an important follow-up question: **When did Alex leave education?** 

This matters because there can be an extension period for Child Benefit:
- If he left education **within the last 20 weeks** (roughly 5 months), you might still be eligible during an extension period
- If it's been **longer than 20 weeks** since he left education, then he wouldn't qualify

**When did Alex finish school or leave his education/training?** (You can give me an approximate date or how many weeks/months ago)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been a while now - he left school last year, so it's been well over 5 months.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Since Alex left school last year (well over 20 weeks ago) and is now working full-time, let me navigate to the appropriate outcome.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Assessment Result

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

### Why Alex doesn't qualify:

**For children aged 16-19**, Child Benefit only continues if they are in **approved education or training**. Since Alex:
- Is 17 years old
- Left education last year (over 20 weeks ago)
- Is now working full-time

He does not meet the qualifying child criteria.

### Important information:

Child Benefit for 16-19 year olds can continue for up to **20 weeks** after they leave education (if they register with a careers service or the armed services). However, since Alex left school over a year ago, this extension period has passed.

### What this means for you:

- If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, it should have stopped when he left education (or 20 weeks after)
- You are not eligible to claim or continue Child Benefit for him while he's in full-time employment

### If circumstances change:

If Alex were to return to **approved education or training** in the future (such as college, A-levels, or approved vocational training), you could become eligible again, as long as he's still under 20 years old.

If you have any questions or if Alex's situation changes, please don't hesitate to reach out again.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which falls within the 16-19 age range where eligibility depends on being in approved education or training. However, Alex left education over 20 weeks ago (last year) and is currently working full-time at a supermarket. Since he is neither in approved education/training nor within the 20-week extension period after leaving education, he does not meet the qualifying child criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 17), who does not qualify as a qualifying child for Child Benefit purposes because he left education over 20 weeks ago and is currently in full-time employment rather than approved education or training.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

response.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
response.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.

However, Alex left education over 20 weeks ago (last year) and is currently working full-time at a supermarket. Since he is neither in approved education/training nor within the 20-week extension period after leaving education, he does not meet the qualifying child criteria for Child Benefit.
